titleStmt.title: Letter from A M Howell to the Chief Secretary's Office, 26 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: A M Howell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
A handwritten letter from A M Howell of Crowborough, Sussex, enclosing a memo requesting information and instructions regarding Mrs Howell's cook, Annie Moriarty, who wishes to visit her home in Co. Cork from her employers home in
An internal Chief Secretary's Office note indicates the intention to inform the sender that a permit was not necessary in the case of a British subject.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers 1916, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5623-15016a

correspAction.placeName: The White House, Crowborough, Sussex, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
26.
Aug.
1916. THE WHITE HOUSE,
CROWBOROUGH,
SUSSEX. Sir, I enclose a query
on a separate Memo
form. I shall be
extremely obliged if
all necessary informa
tion and instructions
may be sent me
in detail. Yours obediently A. M. Howell Inform that a permit to visit
Ireland is not
necessary in the case
of British subjects.
Eof
31.8.16
